living-concrete.knowledge: Campaign (c##) → Quest (q##) → Mission (m##) c##-{name}/ Strategic initiative (global numbering) q##-{name}/ Deliverable goal (per-campaign numbering) m##-{name}/ Tactical execution (per-quest numbering)
BLOCKED = Prerequisites not met | REVEALED = Ready to start ACTIVE = In progress | COMPLETE = Victory | ABANDONED = No longer relevant
Project: campaigns/.alfred.mem | Campaign: c##-*/.alfred.mem Quest: c##-*/q##-*/.alfred.mem | Mission: c##-*/q##-*/m##-*/.alfred.mem
TACTICAL: stays at mission | OPERATIONAL: bubbles to quest STRATEGIC: bubbles to campaign | DOCTRINAL: bubbles to project

README.md = permanent truth (debriefed intel) .alfred.mem = working memory (session-focused) journal/ = autobiographical memory (narrative continuity) Debrief → README. Sleep → .mem. Reflect → journal.

Quest journals are OPTIONAL but valuable for long-running quests.
PROJECT vs QUEST journals: - Project: Breakthroughs that shaped the WHOLE project - Quest: Progress narrative for THIS specific goal
QUEST JOURNAL captures: - Insights that changed quest approach - Tactical pivots and why - Friction points that might recur - Mission retrospectives worth preserving - Quest completion reflections
CONTRACT: {quest}/journal/README.md defines place-specific rules. TEMPLATE: quest_journal_readme_template.md (in quest-forge package)
living-concrete.pattern:
INCLUDE (the tactical special): - Insights that shifted understanding mid-quest - Pivots in approach worth explaining - Friction that future quests might encounter - Mission retrospectives with lasting value - Quest completion/abandonment reflections
EXCLUDE (the operational routine): - Round status (belongs in round reports) - Implementation details (belongs in commits) - Working state (belongs in .mem)
HEURISTIC: "Would someone starting a similar quest benefit from this?"
R{N}.{x} where N=scope, x=continuation (R5.3 = scope 5, round 4)
README.md (charter) | .alfred.mem (shared memory) journal/ (optional, autobiographical - has own README.md contract) discoveries/ (formal discoveries)
## Charter, ## Ideal Solver (5-axis coordinates), ## Loadout (bentos packages) Ideal Solver coords from: campaigns/.knowledge/skill-tree-architecture.md
campaign: c##-{name}/ + README + .alfred.mem quest: q##-{name}/ + README + .alfred.mem [+ journal/] mission: m##-{name}/ + README → execute-mission
map: Full tree with states | active: All ACTIVE | next: Highest-priority REVEALED
